signals.text: Active email thread: Aisha replied Apr 5 excited to work together, agreed to reconnect in ~1 month
Pulkit sent intro email (Apr 3) and Aisha responded positively (Apr 5) - relationship building
Olivia Renfro asked a technical funnel question (Mar 24) - brand is actively using FERMAT
Slack #fermat-x-moment dormant since Mar 3 - last human message was Jan 12 noting low CVR
No upcoming calls scheduled with Moment - brand prefers support-only model
Session volume declining sharply: recent 7d avg ~611/day vs 45d avg ~1,309/day (53% drop)
Moment successfully recovered from churn - renewed at $1,500/month flat fee on 6-month contract
Moment was heads down in fundraising mode (per Aisha Apr 1) - limited bandwidth for optimization
FERMAT automated welcome email sent to Aisha (Apr 1) - onboarding refresh initiated

calls.analysis: One call with Moment in the last 30 days (Mar 18 intro call via Granola with Olivia Renfro and Aisha Chottani). Glyphic has 5 historical Moment/drinkmoment.com calls from Jan 2026 back to Sep 2025 (all with previous CSM Mia DyBuncio). Glyphic call detail/media API returned errors. Account prefers support-only model with no recurring meetings.

calendar.analysis: No Moment-related events found on Pulkit's calendar between Mar 9 - Jun 7 2026. Brand operates on a support-only model without recurring meeting cadence.

slack.analysis: The #fermat-x-moment Slack channel (C05Q4HDFRDE) is dormant. Last non-bot human message was Jan 12 from an unknown user commenting 'wow, cvr is super low' followed by 'why is that?' in response to the weekly roundup. Pulkit joined Feb 24 but has not posted. Weekly Pierre roundup bot posts stopped after Jan 12. Previous CSM Mia's last message was Nov 25 (BFCM coverage). Brand-side participants have not posted in the channel.

messages.subject: Re: Moment / FERMAT
messages.snippet: Sounds great! Let's reconnect in a month once you're through your current priorities. In the meantime, if any questions come up or you'd like to explore specific opportunities on your pages, feel free to reach out anytime.

messages.subject: Your email clicks aren't converting
messages.snippet: Quick question: what happens after someone clicks your email? Most DTC brands send email traffic to generic product pages... FERMAT lets you create custom post-click experiences...

messages.subject: Re: Moment / FERMAT
messages.snippet: Hey Pulkit, Thank you so much, excited to chat! I wanted to know if there is anything in particular you are seeing on our pages that you think needs to be improved? Otherwise, it would be great to connect in about a month or so once we're through a few things on our end.

messages.subject: Re: Moment / FERMAT
messages.snippet: Hi Aisha, Great to meet you! I'm super excited to be working with you and the Moment team as your Customer Success Manager here at FERMAT. I'd love to hop on a call to introduce myself properly.

messages.subject: Your FERMAT journey starts now
messages.snippet: Welcome to FERMAT - we're thrilled to have you on board! Our Brand Support team is here to make sure you hit the ground running.

messages.subject: Re: Moment / FERMAT
messages.snippet: Thanks Isabel, this has been done! Apologies for the delay - we have been heads down in fundraising mode.

messages.subject: Re: Moment / FERMAT
messages.snippet: Hi Aisha, I wanted to touch base, my finance team flagged that the Stripe link sent over last week still hasn't been completed yet. Since your renewal is overdue, can you please complete this by...

messages.subject: Re: Quick Funnel Question
messages.snippet: Hi Olivia, Thanks for reaching out! The Apple Pay button is actually part of the Add to Bag button component, so as of now it's not possible to remove the Add to Bag button...

messages.subject: Quick Funnel Question
messages.snippet: Hi Pulkit and Fermat Team, Quick question on a funnel variant - on mobile, the buy box is currently showing both Add to Bag and Apple Pay buttons. Is there a way to remove the Add to Bag?

email.analysis: Active email engagement. Pulkit introduced himself as new CSM on Apr 3, Aisha responded positively Apr 5, Pulkit replied Apr 7 agreeing to reconnect in a month. Separate thread: Olivia Renfro asked about funnel buy box configuration (Mar 24) and Pulkit answered. Isabel managed Stripe renewal payment (completed Apr 1 after delay due to fundraising). Automated brand support welcome email sent Apr 1. Brand contacts: Aisha Chottani (CEO/primary, aisha@drinkmoment.com), Olivia Renfro (marketing, olivia@drinkmoment.com). Brand is responsive via email but bandwidth-constrained by fundraising.

context_from_other_meetings.detail: Moment had payment/signup issues, coupon code access problems preventing link creation. Account transitioning from Mia to Pulkit.
Moment experiencing ongoing payment and signup issues with coupon code access.
Moment on $10.5k pricing with 6-month contract via Stripe, $1.5k discount applied.
Moment churn recovery successful - Isabel/Mia had CEO discussion and brought them back.
Pulkit designated as point of contact for seller-side support for Moment.

Moment was one of Mia's brand-led accounts. Just renewed $1,500/month flat fee. CEO tries to negotiate discounts, took a full month to confirm renewal. No recurring meetings preferred - support-only model.

account_context.contract: $1,500/month flat fee, 6-month contract via Stripe ($10,500 total, $1,500 discount applied)
account_context.renewal_status: Renewed after churn recovery - Stripe payment completed Apr 1 after delay
account_context.csm_history: Mia DyBuncio (previous CSM) -> Pulkit Srivastava (current CSM, transitioned Feb-Mar 2026)
account_context.communication_preference: Support-only model, no recurring meetings. Prefers email. Brand agreed to reconnect in ~1 month (May 2026).
key_funnels: 3 Reasons Why - Better than Booze (primary, ~95% of traffic)
account_context.notes: Brand was in fundraising mode (per Aisha Apr 1). CEO personality: tries to negotiate discounts, slow on administrative tasks. Successfully recovered from churn in Mar 2026.
